Mountain valleys north of the wolfswood (primary setting)
Greywater Watch (indirectly referenced — "not one of them could find it")
House Whent (implied — "Harrenhal" and "daughter of the great castle")
Night's Watch (the Old Bear led them into the haunted woods; a black brother recruits at Harrenhal feast in story)
Kingsguard / White Swords (in Meera's story — welcoming a new brother; the famous uncle is one)
Ironmen / ironborn (squids in the wolfswood; ironmen dead at Winterfell)
Bolton's bastard's men / flayed men (ride the kingsroad)
The old gods (the crannogman prays to them on the lakeshore; the mystery knight's shield bears a heart tree; "the old gods of north and Neck")
The green men (guardians of the Isle of Faces; dark green skin, leaves for hair, sometimes antlers per Old Nan's stories)
Mountain clans of the North (Wulls, Harclays, Knotts, Liddles, Norreys, Flints)
Crannogmen (the Reeds' people; small folk, queer ways, seldom travel far, sit boats more than horses, hands made for oars not lances)
Andals (referenced as invaders who drowned in the Neck)
First Men (implied through old gods worship and mountain clan culture)
The Liddle's squirrelskin cloak with gold and bronze pinecone clasp
Green and white cloth (Liddle's gift wrapping)
The mystery knight's painted shield (white weirwood with laughing red face)
Tourney sword (used by the she-wolf in the story)
The dragon prince's sad song at the Harrenhal feast (unnamed; "so sad it made the wolf maid sniffle")

mentions.raw_name: Old Nan's stories (referenced as a body of oral tradition)

mentions.raw_name: "Dark wings, dark words" (the Liddle's mother's saying)
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: "Old stories are like old friends. You have to visit them from time to time." (Old Nan's saying, quoted by Bran)
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: Warging (Bran wargs into Summer; sees through Summer's eyes to detect watchers; tries and fails to warg into an eagle)
Greensight / green dreams (Jojen has green dreams — "I dreamed it"; the greenseers could enter birds, Bran believes)

Crannogman magic (in Meera's story — "breathe mud and run on leaves, change earth to water and water to earth with a whispered word, talk to trees and weave words and make castles appear and disappear")
The green men's powers (strange magic; Bran hopes they could help him walk again)
The Ice Dragon constellation (blue star in the dragon's eye points north)
Wolf dreams (Bran gives himself to the wolf dream — experiences midnight through Summer's senses)
"The war" (Theo Wull rode with Howland Reed during it; unnamed in this chapter)
War of the Five Kings (implied — Bolton's bastard alive, flayed men on kingsroad, squids in wolfswood, Stark authority collapsed)
The tourney at Harrenhal (in Meera's story — in "the year of the false spring"; a great tourney with jousting, mêlée, archery, axe-throwing, horse race, tourney of singers)
Prince (Bran is called "Prince Bran" by the Reeds)
Lord (Lord Wull, Lord Manderly, though the Wull is "no true lord")
The Wull, the Knott, the Norrey, the Liddle (mountain clan chief titles — "their own folk" use these rather than "lord")
The Old Bear (title/nickname for the Lord Commander of the Night's Watch)
Queen of love and beauty (tourney title in Meera's story)
Kingsguard / White Swords (order title in Meera's story)
